title: Heating Element Failure
shortDescription: The dishwasher's heating element or its circuit has failed, preventing water from reaching the correct temperature.
fullDescription: Error code E01 indicates a malfunction in the dishwasher's heating system. The control module has detected that the water temperature is not rising as expected during the wash or rinse cycle. This can be caused by a burned-out heating element, a faulty temperature sensor (NTC), a broken relay on the control board, or wiring issues between these components. The dishwasher may complete cycles with cold water, resulting in poor cleaning and sanitization.

causes.cause: Heating element has burned out or developed an open circuit
NTC temperature sensor is faulty or reading incorrectly
Control board relay for the heater has failed
Wiring harness between heater and control board is damaged
Thermal fuse on the heating element has tripped

symptoms: Dishes come out cold after a cycle
Water does not feel warm during wash
Cycle takes much longer than normal
E01 error displayed on control panel
Dishes still have food residue or grease after washing
Sanitize indicator light does not illuminate

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and disconnect power at the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a test cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Access the heating element at the bottom of the tub. Using a multimeter, measure resistance across the heating element terminals. A good element should read between 10-30 ohms. An open circuit (OL) or significantly different reading indicates a failed element.

fixSteps.title: Check the NTC temperature sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor (usually near the sump area). Disconnect it and measure resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature (25C/77F), it should read approximately 50k ohms. Resistance should decrease as temperature increases.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring and connections
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the wiring between the heating element, NTC sensor, and the control board. Look for burned, corroded, or loose connections. Repair or replace damaged wiring.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: If the heating element tested bad, order a replacement. Remove the lower spray arm, unscrew the mounting hardware, disconnect wiring, and install the new element. Reassemble in reverse order.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected or if you are not comfortable working with electrical components near water.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E01 error heating element fix

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher with an E01 error?
faq.a: The dishwasher may still run but will wash with cold water, resulting in poor cleaning and no sanitization. It is best to repair it promptly.

faq.q: How much does it cost to replace a Bosch dishwasher heating element?
faq.a: The heating element itself costs $40-$80 for the part. Professional installation typically adds $100-$200 in labor.

faq.q: Could E01 be a false alarm?
faq.a: Yes, occasionally a power surge or temporary glitch can trigger E01. Try resetting the dishwasher first by disconnecting power for 5 minutes. If the error returns, there is a genuine fault.

title: NTC Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor is providing out-of-range readings to the control board.
fullDescription: Error code E02 signals that the NTC (Negative Temperature Coefficient) sensor used to monitor water temperature is malfunctioning. The control board is receiving readings that are outside the expected range, either too high, too low, or erratic. This prevents the dishwasher from properly regulating water temperature during wash and rinse cycles.

causes.cause: NTC temperature sensor has failed
Wiring to the NTC sensor is broken or corroded
Connector on the control board is loose or oxidized
Control board input circuit is damaged

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dishwasher from power for 5 minutes. Reconnect and start a cycle. If E02 returns, proceed to further diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The NTC sensor is typically located in the sump area at the bottom of the dishwasher. You may need to remove the lower rack and spray arm to access the sump cover.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the NTC sensor harness and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read around 50k ohms. Place it in warm water and verify resistance decreases smoothly. Erratic or fixed readings mean the sensor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, replace it. Disconnect the old sensor, remove from the sump housing, insert the new sensor, and reconnect the wiring harness. Run a test cycle to confirm the error is resolved.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the sensor does not fix the issue, the control board may need diagnosis or replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E02 error NTC sensor replacement

faq.q: What does the NTC sensor do in a dishwasher?
faq.a: The NTC sensor monitors water temperature so the control board can regulate heating. Without accurate readings, the dishwasher cannot heat water properly.

faq.q: Is E02 the same as E01?
faq.a: No. E01 typically relates to the heater itself while E02 specifically points to the temperature sensor. However, both affect water heating.

faq.q: Can a faulty NTC sensor damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: It will not cause physical damage, but without proper temperature regulation, cleaning performance will suffer and sanitization may be compromised.

title: Fill Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher failed to fill with enough water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error E03 is triggered when the dishwasher's control board detects that the tub has not filled with adequate water within the allotted time period (typically 5-8 minutes). The water level sensor or pressure switch indicates insufficient water despite the fill valve being activated. This could be due to water supply issues, a faulty fill valve, or a malfunctioning water level sensor.

causes.cause: Water supply valve to the dishwasher is closed or restricted
Water inlet valve is clogged or defective
Inlet hose is kinked or blocked
Water level sensor or pressure switch is faulty
Float switch is stuck in the raised position

symptoms: Dishwasher starts but stops shortly after
No water sound during fill phase
Very little water visible in the tub
Cycle aborts within the first few minutes

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the water supply valve under the sink is fully open. Run water from the kitchen faucet to confirm adequate water pressure and flow to the area.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and check the water inlet hose for kinks, bends, or blockages. Straighten if kinked. Disconnect and flush if blocked.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the water line from the inlet valve at the back of the dishwasher. Remove the small mesh filter screen and clean any debris or mineral buildup. Replace the screen and reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, test the inlet valve solenoid coil. It should read between 500-1500 ohms. If it reads open (OL) or shorted, replace the valve.

fixSteps.title: Check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door and locate the float assembly on the tub floor (usually front-left). Lift and lower it manually. It should move freely. If stuck, clean around it to remove any debris.

whenToCallPro: If the water supply and inlet valve are fine, the issue may be with the control board or pressure sensor, which require professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E03 error not filling water fix

faq.q: Why does my Bosch dishwasher show E03?
faq.a: E03 means the dishwasher did not fill with enough water in the expected time. Check that the water supply is on and the inlet hose is not kinked.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause E03?
faq.a: Yes. Bosch dishwashers require a minimum of about 20 PSI water pressure. If your home water pressure is low, this can trigger E03.

faq.q: How do I reset E03 on my Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: Turn off the dishwasher and disconnect power for 5 minutes. After restoring power, the error should clear if the underlying issue is resolved.

title: Water Flow Sensor Error
shortDescription: The flow sensor has detected irregular or no water flow through the spray system.
fullDescription: Error E04 indicates that the water flow sensor is not detecting proper flow through the spray arms and circulation system. This can mean the circulation pump is not moving water correctly, spray arms are blocked, or the flow sensor itself is defective. The dishwasher relies on this sensor to confirm water is being distributed properly during the wash cycle.

causes.cause: Flow sensor is defective or clogged with debris
Spray arm nozzles are blocked with food particles or mineral deposits
Circulation pump impeller is damaged or jammed
Wiring to the flow sensor is damaged

symptoms: Dishes not getting clean despite running full cycle
Weak or no spray from spray arms
Unusual humming from the circulation pump
Cycle completes but dishes are still dirty

fixSteps.title: Clean the spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove both upper and lower spray arms by unscrewing or unclipping them. Rinse under running water and use a toothpick to clear each spray nozzle of debris and mineral buildup.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cylindrical fine filter and flat coarse filter from the bottom of the tub. Rinse thoroughly under running water. Scrub with a soft brush to remove grease and food particles.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the flow sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow sensor on the circulation pump assembly (usually a small turbine-style sensor). Remove it and check for debris or scale buildup. Clean the sensor and its housing. Verify the impeller spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Check the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher running, listen for the circulation pump. If it hums but does not move water, the impeller may be jammed or broken. Access the pump from underneath and inspect the impeller for damage.

whenToCallPro: If the circulation pump needs replacement, a professional can ensure proper installation and test the system.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E04 error flow sensor fix

faq.q: What is the flow sensor in a Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: The flow sensor is a small turbine device that measures water flow through the circulation system. It tells the control board that water is being properly distributed.

faq.q: Can clogged filters cause E04?
faq.a: Yes, severely clogged filters can restrict water flow enough to trigger E04. Regular filter cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: How often should I clean my Bosch dishwasher filters?
faq.a: Bosch recommends cleaning the filters at least once a month, or more frequently if you wash heavily soiled dishes without pre-rinsing.

title: Water Level Overfill Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected an overfill condition with too much water in the tub.
fullDescription: Error E05 is triggered when the water level sensor or pressure switch detects that the water in the tub has exceeded the maximum safe level. This overfill protection activates the drain pump to prevent water from overflowing. The cause is typically a stuck-open inlet valve, a faulty water level sensor, or a drain issue that is allowing backflow into the tub.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is stuck open and not shutting off
Water level pressure switch or sensor is malfunctioning
Drain hose is connected too low, causing siphoning backflow
Control board relay for the inlet valve is shorted

symptoms: Water visible above the bottom of the door
Water leaking from the dishwasher door
Dishwasher fills then immediately starts draining

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply valve under the sink to prevent further filling. Then disconnect power to the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose installation
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the drain hose has a high loop (at least 20 inches above the floor) or is connected to an air gap on the sink. Without a high loop, dirty water from the sink can siphon back into the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Restore power briefly and listen at the inlet valve. If you hear water flowing when the dishwasher is off or between cycles, the valve is stuck open and must be replaced. Shut off water supply immediately if this is the case.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water, disconnect the supply line from the valve. Remove the mounting screws and disconnect the electrical connector. Install the new valve in reverse order.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is not the issue, the control board may need professional diagnosis for a shorted relay.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E05 overfill error fix

faq.q: Is an E05 error dangerous?
faq.a: It can lead to water overflow and potential water damage. Turn off the water supply immediately and address the issue before running the dishwasher again.

faq.q: What is a high loop for a dishwasher drain?
faq.a: A high loop means securing the drain hose as high as possible under the counter before it connects to the drain. This prevents dirty water from siphoning back into the dishwasher.

faq.q: Can E05 cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes. If the inlet valve is stuck open and the overfill protection fails, the dishwasher can overflow. Always shut off the water supply when this error appears.

title: Door Switch or Latch Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot confirm that the door is properly closed and latched.
fullDescription: Error E06 indicates a problem with the door latch mechanism or door switch. The control board requires confirmation that the door is securely latched before starting or continuing a cycle. If the door switch does not send the correct signal, the dishwasher will not operate and will display E06.

causes.cause: Door latch assembly is worn or broken
Door switch is defective
Door hinge or spring is damaged, preventing proper closure
Wiring to the door switch is loose or damaged

symptoms: Door appears closed but dishwasher does not recognize it
Clicking sound when attempting to start

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door latch
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and examine the latch mechanism on the door and the strike plate on the tub. Look for visible damage, wear, or misalignment. Ensure the latch clicks firmly when closing.

fixSteps.title: Test the door switch
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the door switch by removing the inner door panel. With a multimeter, test for continuity when the switch is activated. It should show continuity when pressed and no continuity when released.

fixSteps.title: Check door alignment
fixSteps.instruction: Close the door slowly and observe if it aligns properly with the tub opening. If the door is sagging, the hinge springs may need adjustment or replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the inner door panel screws. Disconnect the latch electrical connector. Remove mounting screws and the old latch. Install the new latch and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: If the latch and switch are fine but the error persists, the control board may have an issue requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E06 door latch error fix

faq.q: Why does my Bosch dishwasher say E06 when the door is closed?
faq.a: Even if the door appears closed, the electrical door switch may not be making contact. The switch or latch mechanism may be worn and need replacement.

faq.q: Can I bypass the door switch?
faq.a: No. The door switch is a safety device that prevents the dishwasher from operating with the door open. Bypassing it is dangerous and could cause water damage or injury.

faq.q: How do I access the door switch on a Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: Remove the T20 Torx screws around the inner door panel. Carefully separate the inner panel from the outer door to access the latch and switch assembly.

title: Drying Fan Error
shortDescription: The drying fan motor is not operating or is running at incorrect speed.
fullDescription: Error E07 indicates a problem with the dishwasher's drying fan (on models with a fan-assisted drying system). The control board cannot detect the fan running or detects abnormal fan speed. This affects the drying performance but the dishwasher may still wash normally.

causes.cause: Drying fan motor has failed
Fan blade is obstructed by debris
Wiring to the fan motor is disconnected or damaged
Control board output for the fan has failed

symptoms: No fan noise during drying phase
Excessive moisture and condensation after cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes, restore, and run a cycle. If E07 returns, proceed with diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Locate and inspect the fan
fixSteps.instruction: The drying fan is typically located on the inside of the door panel or near the top of the tub. Remove the inner door panel or access panel to inspect the fan for obstructions or visible damage.

fixSteps.title: Test the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the fan motor wires and use a multimeter to check resistance across the motor terminals. A good motor typically reads 20-100 ohms. Also check for 120V AC at the connector during the drying phase to verify the control board is sending power.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor has failed, remove the mounting screws, disconnect the wiring, and install the new fan motor. Ensure the fan blade spins freely after installation.

whenToCallPro: If the fan motor tests fine but receives no power, the control board may need professional repair.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E07 drying fan error fix

faq.q: Can I still wash dishes with E07?
faq.a: Yes, the dishwasher will still wash dishes normally. Only the drying function is affected. Dishes will need to be towel-dried or air-dried with the door open.

faq.q: Does every Bosch dishwasher have a drying fan?
faq.a: No. Some models use condensation drying with no fan. E07 only applies to models with fan-assisted drying systems, typically the higher-end 800 and Benchmark series.

faq.q: Why are my dishes not drying in my Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: Poor drying can be caused by E07 fan issues, but also by not using rinse aid, overloading, or using low-temperature cycles. Check rinse aid levels first.

title: Low Water Level in Sump
shortDescription: Insufficient water detected in the sump area during the wash cycle.
fullDescription: Error E08 signals that the water level in the sump (the recirculation area at the bottom of the tub) is too low during the wash cycle. The circulation pump needs adequate water to operate properly. This can indicate a slow leak, a fill problem, or an issue with the water level sensing system.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve is partially clogged, delivering insufficient water
Small leak in the sump seal or hose connections
Water level sensor is miscalibrated or faulty
Circulation pump seal is leaking

symptoms: Washing sounds seem different (gurgling or sucking air)
Small amounts of water under the dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out and inspect underneath for any signs of water leakage. Check all hose connections, the sump area, and the door gasket.

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply valve is fully open and the inlet hose has no kinks. Run water from the kitchen faucet to verify adequate pressure.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the water supply hose from the inlet valve. Remove and clean the mesh filter screen of any mineral deposits or debris.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sump and pump seals
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sump area from underneath. Check the pump seals and hose clamps for any signs of leakage. Tighten loose clamps and replace damaged seals.

whenToCallPro: If you find water under the dishwasher but cannot locate the source, a professional can pressure-test the system.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E08 low water error fix

faq.q: What causes low water in the dishwasher sump?
faq.a: The most common cause is a partially clogged inlet valve filter restricting water flow. Less commonly, a small leak can cause water to slowly drain from the sump.

faq.q: Can I add water manually to fix E08?
faq.a: Adding water manually may temporarily clear the error, but it does not address the root cause. The fill system or a leak needs to be repaired.

faq.q: Is E08 the same as E03?
faq.a: They are related but different. E03 indicates the dishwasher could not fill at all within the timeout, while E08 indicates water level dropped too low during a cycle.

title: Heating Circuit Error
shortDescription: The control board has detected an open or short circuit in the heating system.
fullDescription: Error E09 is a more specific heating fault than E01. It indicates the control board has detected an electrical fault in the heating circuit, such as an open circuit (broken connection) or short circuit. This is distinct from a simple heater failure in that the control board is detecting an abnormal electrical condition rather than just a failure to heat.

causes.cause: Heating element has a short circuit or ground fault
Wiring between control board and heater is damaged
Control board triac or relay for heating has failed
Moisture intrusion on heater connections causing short

symptoms: Dishwasher immediately stops when trying to heat
Circuit breaker may trip when dishwasher runs
Burning smell from the dishwasher (in case of short circuit)

fixSteps.title: Safety first - disconnect power
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dishwasher from power at the circuit breaker. Do not reconnect until the issue is diagnosed. If you noticed a burning smell, do not run the unit.

fixSteps.title: Test heater for ground fault
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access the heating element terminals. Using a multimeter, test between each heater terminal and the metal chassis. Any reading other than OL (open line) indicates a ground fault and the heater must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Test heater resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the two heater terminals. Normal reading is 10-30 ohms. A reading of 0 ohms indicates a short, OL indicates an open circuit. Both require replacement.

fixSteps.title: Inspect wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Check all wiring between the control board and heater for signs of burning, melting, or damage. Pay special attention to connectors for discoloration or corrosion. Replace any damaged wiring.

whenToCallPro: If you detect a burning smell or the circuit breaker trips when the dishwasher runs, call a professional immediately. Electrical shorts can be dangerous.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E09 heating circuit error fix

faq.q: Is E09 dangerous?
faq.a: A short circuit in the heating element can potentially trip your breaker or in rare cases cause overheating. Disconnect power and do not run the dishwasher until repaired.

faq.q: What is the difference between E01 and E09?
faq.a: E01 typically means the heater is not heating (could be dead element or sensor), while E09 specifically detects an electrical fault like a short or ground fault in the heating circuit.

faq.q: Can moisture cause E09?
faq.a: Yes. If water gets into the heater electrical connections, it can create a ground fault that triggers E09. Drying the connections may temporarily fix it, but the source of moisture should be found.

title: Heating Timeout Error
shortDescription: The water did not reach the target temperature within the maximum allowed heating time.
fullDescription: Error E10 occurs when the dishwasher's water fails to reach the programmed temperature within the control board's timeout period. Unlike E01 (heater failure) or E09 (heating circuit fault), E10 specifically means the heater is operating but cannot achieve the target temperature in time. This often points to partial heater degradation, very cold incoming water, or thermal losses.

causes.cause: Heating element is partially degraded, heating slowly
Incoming water temperature is extremely cold
NTC sensor is reading slightly inaccurately
Limescale buildup on the heating element reducing efficiency

symptoms: Cycle takes much longer than usual before error appears
Water is warm but not hot
Dishwasher appears to work but eventually stops with error
Works on cooler wash settings but fails on hot settings

fixSteps.title: Run a descaling cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Place a dishwasher descaling agent (like Bosch Descaler) in the empty tub and run a hot cycle. Limescale on the heater reduces efficiency and can cause heating timeouts.

fixSteps.title: Check incoming water temperature
fixSteps.instruction: Run the hot water at the kitchen sink until it is hot. Measure with a thermometer. If incoming hot water is below 120F/49C, the dishwasher has to work harder. Consider connecting to the hot water supply.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance across the heater terminals. A partially degraded element may read higher than the normal 10-30 ohms. If resistance is significantly higher (over 50 ohms), the element is degrading and should be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Replace heating element if needed
fixSteps.instruction: If the element tests out of spec, replace it. Remove the lower spray arm, access the element mounting, disconnect wiring, remove the old element, and install the new one.

repairTime: 15 minutes to 1.5 hours depending on cause
whenToCallPro: If descaling and element replacement do not resolve the issue, there may be a control board timing problem requiring professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E10 heating timeout error fix

faq.q: Can hard water cause E10?
faq.a: Yes. Hard water creates limescale buildup on the heating element, reducing its efficiency and causing it to take longer to heat water, eventually triggering E10.

faq.q: Should I connect my dishwasher to hot or cold water?
faq.a: Bosch recommends connecting to the hot water supply (up to 140F/60C). This reduces the heating burden on the internal element and can prevent E10.

faq.q: How often should I descale my Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: In hard water areas, descale every 1-3 months. In areas with soft water, every 6 months is sufficient. Regular descaling prevents E10 errors.

title: NTC Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor circuit is reading as open (disconnected).
fullDescription: Error E11 specifically indicates that the control board detects an open circuit condition on the NTC temperature sensor input. This means the sensor wire is broken, the connector is disconnected, or the sensor element itself has failed open. Without temperature feedback, the dishwasher cannot regulate heating.

causes.cause: NTC sensor connector has come loose
NTC sensor element has failed open
Wire to the NTC sensor is broken
Control board connector pin is corroded

symptoms: Dishwasher will not start or stops immediately

fixSteps.title: Check NTC sensor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the sump area and locate the NTC sensor connector. Ensure it is firmly seated. Disconnect and reconnect to reseat the pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the NTC sensor and measure resistance. At room temperature it should read approximately 50k ohms. If it reads OL (open), the sensor has failed.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring continuity
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests good, check continuity of each wire from the sensor connector to the control board connector. A broken wire will read OL.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is open, remove it from the sump housing and install a replacement. Reconnect wiring and test.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor and wiring test fine, the control board input may be damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E11 NTC sensor open circuit fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E11 and E02?
faq.a: E11 specifically indicates an open circuit (no connection), while E02 can indicate any out-of-range reading including short circuits or drift.

faq.q: Can vibration cause E11?
faq.a: Yes, dishwasher vibration can loosen connectors over time. Reseating the NTC connector is the first thing to try.

faq.q: Is the NTC sensor expensive to replace?
faq.a: No, the NTC sensor is one of the least expensive dishwasher parts, typically $15-$35.

title: NTC Sensor Short Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC temperature sensor circuit is reading as shorted.
fullDescription: Error E12 indicates the control board detects a short circuit on the NTC sensor input. The resistance reading is near zero instead of the expected 50k ohms at room temperature. This could be caused by a shorted sensor, water intrusion into the connector, or damaged wiring with shorted conductors.

causes.cause: NTC sensor has internally shorted
Water or moisture in the NTC connector
Sensor wiring is damaged with conductors touching
Corrosion on connector pins creating a bridge

symptoms: Heater may run continuously if sensor shorts to low resistance
Water may get extremely hot before error trips
Steam or excess heat from dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power immediately
fixSteps.instruction: If the dishwasher is producing excessive heat or steam, disconnect power at the breaker immediately. A shorted sensor can cause uncontrolled heating.

fixSteps.title: Dry the NTC connector
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the NTC sensor connector. Disconnect it and inspect for moisture. Dry thoroughly with compressed air or let air-dry. Clean any corrosion on the pins with electrical contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: With the sensor disconnected, measure resistance. At room temperature it should be about 50k ohms. A reading near 0 ohms confirms a short. Replace the sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the shorted sensor and install a new one. Ensure the connector is properly sealed against moisture.

whenToCallPro: If you experienced overheating or steam, have a professional inspect for any heat damage to components before restarting.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E12 NTC sensor short circuit fix

faq.q: Is a shorted NTC sensor dangerous?
faq.a: Potentially yes. A shorted sensor can cause the control board to think the water is cold when it is actually very hot, leading to uncontrolled heating. Disconnect power promptly.

faq.q: Why would moisture get into the sensor connector?
faq.a: Splash from the wash cycle, a small leak, or condensation can allow moisture into electrical connectors. Proper routing and securing of wires helps prevent this.

faq.q: Can I test the NTC sensor without removing it?
faq.a: You can test resistance at the connector without removing the sensor from its housing. Just disconnect the plug and measure across the sensor pins.

title: Water Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The incoming water temperature exceeds the dishwasher's maximum allowed temperature.
fullDescription: Error E13 indicates that the water entering the dishwasher is too hot, exceeding approximately 167F/75C. This is a safety protection to prevent damage to internal components and potential scalding. The most common cause is that the household water heater is set too high.

causes.cause: Household water heater is set above 150F/65C
NTC sensor is reading incorrectly (too low), causing false high-temp reading
Mixing valve on the water supply is malfunctioning
Heater element is stuck on due to control board fault

symptoms: Dishwasher stops at beginning of cycle
Hot water supply at the sink is extremely hot

fixSteps.title: Check water heater setting
fixSteps.instruction: Check your household water heater thermostat. It should be set to 120F/49C for optimal dishwasher performance. Reduce if set higher than 140F/60C.

fixSteps.title: Run cold water
fixSteps.instruction: Run the hot water at the kitchen sink until it cools to a moderate temperature. Then start the dishwasher. Very hot water sitting in pipes near the dishwasher can trigger E13.

fixSteps.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If water temperature is normal, the NTC sensor may be reading low (telling the board the water is hotter than it is). Test the sensor resistance and compare to the expected value.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: After adjusting the water heater, disconnect dishwasher power for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a test cycle.

whenToCallPro: If the water heater is set correctly and the NTC sensor tests fine, there may be a control board issue causing the heater to run continuously.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E13 water too hot error fix

faq.q: What temperature should my water heater be set to?
faq.a: For optimal dishwasher performance, set your water heater to 120F/49C. Bosch dishwashers have internal heaters to boost temperature as needed.

faq.q: Can E13 damage my dishes?
faq.a: Excessively hot water can damage delicate items like plastics, glassware, and items with gold or silver trim. Address E13 promptly.

faq.q: Is E13 a safety feature?
faq.a: Yes, E13 protects both the dishwasher's internal components and your dishes from excessively hot water.

title: Flow Meter Error
shortDescription: The water flow meter is not detecting water flow during the fill cycle.
fullDescription: Error E14 indicates that the flow meter (reed switch or hall effect sensor) that counts incoming water volume is not registering pulses during the fill cycle. The control board uses this meter to measure exact water volume entering the tub. A stuck or faulty flow meter, or debris blocking the sensor, causes this error.

causes.cause: Flow meter sensor is faulty or stuck
Debris blocking the flow meter impeller
Wiring to the flow meter is damaged
Very low water pressure preventing impeller rotation

symptoms: Cycle does not advance past fill stage

fixSteps.title: Check water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the water supply valve is fully open. Run the kitchen faucet to confirm good flow and pressure.

fixSteps.title: Clean the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the flow meter (usually in the inlet assembly). Remove it and clean any debris from the impeller. Ensure it spins freely.

fixSteps.title: Test the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: Use a multimeter to check the flow meter's electrical output. The sensor should produce pulses when the impeller spins. If there is no output with the impeller spinning, the sensor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the flow meter
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve the issue, replace the flow meter assembly. Disconnect the wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new unit.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the flow meter does not resolve the error, the control board input may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E14 flow meter error fix

faq.q: What does the flow meter do?
faq.a: The flow meter precisely measures the volume of water entering the dishwasher. This ensures the correct amount of water is used for each cycle.

faq.q: Can mineral deposits affect the flow meter?
faq.a: Yes, hard water deposits can slow or stop the flow meter impeller. Regular descaling helps prevent this.

faq.q: Will E14 cause flooding?
faq.a: If the flow meter fails, the dishwasher may overfill because it cannot measure incoming water volume. The overfill protection (E05) should activate as a secondary safeguard.

title: Anti-Flood Protection Activated (AquaStop)
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base pan, activating the Bosch AquaStop anti-flood system.
fullDescription: Error E15 is one of the most common Bosch dishwasher errors. It indicates that water has accumulated in the base pan beneath the dishwasher tub, triggering the AquaStop float switch. This is a critical safety feature that prevents flooding. The drain pump typically runs continuously when E15 is active. The water in the base pan must be removed and the source of the leak identified before the dishwasher can operate again.

causes.cause: Leaking door seal or gasket
Loose or cracked sump hose connection
Spray arm seal leaking
Cracked sump or wash tub
Leaking water inlet valve

symptoms: Drain pump runs continuously even when off
Dishwasher will not start any cycle

fixSteps.title: Disconnect power and water
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher, disconnect power at the breaker, and shut off the water supply valve under the sink.

fixSteps.title: Tilt to drain the base pan
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pull the dishwasher out from under the counter. Tilt the dishwasher forward approximately 45 degrees over towels to drain accumulated water from the base pan. Alternatively, use towels or a wet-dry vacuum to soak up the water.

fixSteps.title: Identify the leak source
fixSteps.instruction: With the base pan dry, slide the dishwasher back and reconnect power and water. Run a cycle and watch from underneath with a flashlight for the leak source. Common locations: door gasket, sump connections, spray arm seals, inlet valve.

fixSteps.title: Fix the leak
fixSteps.instruction: Based on the leak source: replace the door gasket if torn, tighten or replace hose clamps if a connection is loose, replace the spray arm seal if leaking, or replace the inlet valve if dripping.

fixSteps.title: Dry and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the base pan is completely dry. The float switch must return to its lowered position. Reconnect everything and test. E15 should clear once the base pan is dry.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot locate the leak source, or if the tub or sump housing is cracked, professional repair or replacement may be necessary.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E15 AquaStop error water in base fix

faq.q: Why does my Bosch dishwasher keep showing E15?
faq.a: E15 means water is in the base pan. Even after clearing the error, it will return if the underlying leak is not fixed. You must find and repair the leak source.

faq.q: Can I reset E15 without tilting the dishwasher?
faq.a: You can try using towels or a wet-dry vacuum to absorb water from the base pan through the front bottom panel. However, tilting is usually the most effective method.

faq.q: Why does the pump keep running with E15?
faq.a: The continuous pump operation is by design. When E15 triggers, the drain pump runs to remove as much water as possible from the base pan to prevent flooding.

title: Unauthorized Water Fill
shortDescription: The dishwasher detects water filling when no fill command was issued.
fullDescription: Error E16 indicates water is entering the dishwasher when the control board has not activated the fill valve. This means the inlet valve is leaking or stuck partially open, allowing water to enter without authorization. This is a safety concern as uncontrolled water entry could lead to overfilling and flooding.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve diaphragm is worn and leaking through
Debris preventing the inlet valve from fully closing
Siphoning from drain hose (no high loop)
Control board sending spurious fill signals

symptoms: Water sound heard when dishwasher is idle
Water accumulates in tub between cycles
Dishes found sitting in water after long standby

fixSteps.title: Shut off water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply valve to the dishwasher under the sink. This stops any further unauthorized filling.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose high loop
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the drain hose has a high loop under the counter or connects through an air gap. Without this, sink water can siphon into the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Test the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With water supply off and the tub empty, turn the water supply back on briefly. Listen at the valve. If you hear water flowing when the dishwasher is off, the valve is leaking through and must be replaced.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water, disconnect the supply line, remove the valve's mounting screws and electrical connector. Install the new valve and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve is new and the issue persists, the control board may be sending erratic signals and needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E16 unauthorized fill error valve fix

faq.q: Should I leave the water supply on when the dishwasher is not in use?
faq.a: If E16 has occurred, turn off the supply between uses until the valve is replaced. Normally, the supply can stay on.

faq.q: Can a leaking inlet valve cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes, if the valve leaks continuously while the dishwasher is off, the tub can overfill and eventually overflow. E16 and the AquaStop system help prevent this.

faq.q: How long does an inlet valve last?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 years depending on water quality. Hard water and sediment accelerate wear on the valve diaphragm.

title: Water Inlet Valve Error
shortDescription: The water inlet valve is not responding properly to control board commands.
fullDescription: Error E17 indicates a general water inlet valve malfunction. The control board has sent a command to open the valve but is not getting the expected response. This could mean the valve solenoid has failed, the wiring is damaged, or the valve is mechanically stuck.

causes.cause: Inlet valve solenoid coil has burned out
Electrical connector to the valve is corroded or loose
Control board output for the valve has failed
Valve is mechanically seized from mineral buildup

symptoms: No clicking sound from the inlet valve when cycle starts
Dishwasher starts drain pump then stops
Repeated fill attempts with no water

fixSteps.title: Verify water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Confirm the water supply valve is fully open and other fixtures have normal water flow.

fixSteps.title: Check valve connector
fixSteps.instruction: Access the water inlet valve (usually bottom-left behind the kick plate). Disconnect and reconnect the electrical connector. Check for corrosion on the pins.

fixSteps.title: Test the valve solenoid
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the valve connector. Measure resistance across the solenoid coil terminals with a multimeter. Normal range is 500-1500 ohms. OL or very low readings indicate a failed solenoid.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Shut off water supply. Disconnect the water line and electrical connector. Remove mounting screws. Install the new valve and reconnect everything.

whenToCallPro: If the valve tests fine electrically and mechanically, the control board output needs professional testing.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E17 inlet valve error fix

faq.q: How do I know if my inlet valve is bad?
faq.a: If the solenoid resistance is outside 500-1500 ohms, or if the valve does not click when 120V is applied, it needs replacement.

faq.q: Can I test the inlet valve without removing it?
faq.a: You can test the solenoid electrically by disconnecting the wires and measuring resistance. To test mechanically, you would need to apply voltage which is best left to a technician.

faq.q: Is E17 the same as E03?
faq.a: They are related. E03 indicates a fill timeout (not enough water within a time limit), while E17 specifically points to the inlet valve not responding to commands.

title: Low Water Pressure Error
shortDescription: Insufficient water pressure detected at the inlet, preventing proper filling.
fullDescription: Error E18 indicates that the water pressure at the dishwasher's inlet is below the minimum required threshold (approximately 20 PSI). The flow meter detects that water is entering too slowly to fill the tub within the timeout period. This can be a household plumbing issue or a restriction in the dishwasher's supply line.

causes.cause: Water supply valve is not fully open
Inlet hose filter screen is clogged with sediment
Household water pressure is too low
Kinked or partially blocked supply hose

symptoms: Dishwasher takes very long to fill
Weak flow from kitchen faucet when dishwasher fills
Error appears more often during peak water usage times
Cycle may complete on some attempts but not others

fixSteps.title: Open water supply fully
fixSteps.instruction: Check the shut-off valve under the sink and ensure it is fully open. Turn it counterclockwise until it stops.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet filter screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the supply hose from the dishwasher inlet. Remove the small mesh filter screen and clean it of any debris, sediment, or mineral buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check hose for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the full length of the water supply hose for kinks, sharp bends, or compression. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test water pressure
fixSteps.instruction: Use a water pressure gauge on an outdoor spigot or the kitchen line. Minimum recommended pressure is 20 PSI. If below this, a plumber may need to address household pressure issues or a booster pump may be needed.

whenToCallPro: If household water pressure is consistently below 20 PSI, a plumber should evaluate your home's water supply system.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E18 low water pressure error fix

faq.q: What water pressure does a Bosch dishwasher need?
faq.a: Bosch dishwashers require a minimum of approximately 20 PSI and a maximum of 145 PSI water pressure.

faq.q: Can E18 happen intermittently?
faq.a: Yes. If your home water pressure fluctuates (e.g., when multiple fixtures run simultaneously), E18 may appear only sometimes.

faq.q: How do I test my home water pressure?
faq.a: Attach a water pressure gauge to an outdoor hose bib or the kitchen supply line. Normal residential pressure is 40-80 PSI.

title: Diverter Valve Error
shortDescription: The water diverter valve that directs flow between spray arms is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error E19 indicates the diverter valve (which switches water flow between the upper and lower spray arms and ceiling sprayer) is not functioning correctly. The control board cannot confirm the valve is moving to the commanded position. This results in uneven or poor cleaning as water is not properly distributed.

causes.cause: Diverter valve motor has failed
Diverter valve is stuck due to food debris or scale
Wiring to the diverter valve is damaged
Position sensor on the diverter valve is faulty

symptoms: Only top or bottom rack gets clean
Clicking or grinding noise during cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 5 minutes. Sometimes the diverter valve position gets out of sync and a reset allows it to recalibrate.

fixSteps.title: Clean the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: Run a cleaning cycle with Bosch dishwasher cleaner or white vinegar to dissolve any scale or grease that may be causing the valve to stick.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: Access the diverter valve in the sump area. Check that it moves freely. Remove any debris blocking the valve mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the valve motor or position sensor is faulty, replace the diverter valve assembly. Disconnect wiring, remove mounting hardware, and install the new assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the diverter valve is accessible but the motor or sensor needs precise alignment, a technician can ensure proper calibration.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E19 diverter valve error fix

faq.q: What does the diverter valve do?
faq.a: The diverter valve alternates water flow between the lower spray arm, upper spray arm, and (on some models) the ceiling sprayer for comprehensive cleaning.

faq.q: Why is only one rack getting clean?
faq.a: A stuck diverter valve sends water to only one spray arm zone. E19 confirms this as the cause.

faq.q: Can I manually move the diverter valve?
faq.a: It should move freely when the dishwasher is off and unpowered. If it feels stuck, it likely has debris or scale buildup.

title: Circulation Pump Motor Error
shortDescription: The main circulation pump motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: Error E20 indicates a fault with the main circulation pump motor (also called the wash motor). This is the primary pump that circulates water through the spray arms during the wash and rinse cycles. The control board has detected that the motor is not starting, running at incorrect speed, or drawing abnormal current.

causes.cause: Circulation pump motor has burned out
Pump impeller is jammed by glass or debris
Capacitor for the pump motor has failed
Wiring or connector to the pump motor is damaged

symptoms: No water spray during cycle (silence instead of wash sounds)
Humming or buzzing from the pump area
Dishwasher fills with water but does not wash

fixSteps.title: Check for impeller obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower spray arm, cylindrical filter, and flat filter. Check for any debris (broken glass, bones, toothpicks) that may have fallen into the sump and jammed the pump impeller.

fixSteps.title: Verify pump motor operation
fixSteps.instruction: With filters cleaned and reassembled, run a cycle and listen. You should hear the motor start and water flowing. A hum without water flow indicates a jam or seized motor.

fixSteps.title: Test the pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Access the pump motor from underneath. Disconnect the wiring and test resistance across the motor terminals. Typical readings are 5-20 ohms. OL means an open winding. Also check for ground faults between terminals and motor housing.

fixSteps.title: Replace the circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the motor is faulty, the pump assembly typically needs to be replaced as a unit. This involves disconnecting hoses, wiring, and mounting hardware from underneath the tub.

whenToCallPro: Circulation pump replacement requires working underneath the dishwasher with plumbing and electrical connections. Professional installation is recommended for most homeowners.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E20 circulation pump motor error fix

faq.q: How much does a Bosch dishwasher circulation pump cost?
faq.a: The pump assembly typically costs $80-$180 for the part. Professional installation adds $120-$250 in labor.

faq.q: Can I replace just the motor without the pump?
faq.a: In most Bosch models, the motor and pump are sold as an integrated assembly. Individual motor replacement is not typically possible.

faq.q: What causes the circulation pump to fail?
faq.a: Common causes include debris damage to the impeller, bearing wear over time, voltage spikes, and age-related motor winding breakdown.

title: Drain Pump Error
shortDescription: The drain pump is not operating correctly or cannot evacuate water from the tub.
fullDescription: Error E21 indicates the drain pump motor has failed or cannot effectively remove water from the dishwasher tub. The control board detects that the pump is not running, running but not draining, or drawing excessive current. This results in standing water in the tub after a cycle.

causes.cause: Drain pump impeller is blocked by debris
Drain pump motor has failed
Drain hose is clogged or kinked
Garbage disposal knockout plug was not removed
Drain pump wiring is damaged

symptoms: Standing water in the bottom of the tub after cycle
Water backs up into the dishwasher from the sink

fixSteps.title: Check the drain filter and sump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filters. Check the drain area for debris such as food particles, glass, or plastic that could block the drain pump.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the drain hose for kinks. If connected to a garbage disposal, ensure the knockout plug was removed during installation. Check the hose connection at the sink drain or disposal.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Access the drain pump from underneath. Disconnect the wiring and measure resistance. A functional pump motor typically reads 100-300 ohms. Check that the impeller turns freely.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump motor has failed, disconnect the drain hose, wiring, and mounting clips. Remove the old pump and install the new one. Reconnect everything and test.

whenToCallPro: If you are unable to access the drain pump or if the drain connects to complex plumbing, a professional can diagnose and repair efficiently.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E21 drain pump error fix

faq.q: What if I just had a garbage disposal installed?
faq.a: A very common cause of drain issues after a new disposal is the knockout plug not being removed. Check inside the disposal where the dishwasher drain hose connects.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes, bail out standing water with a cup and towels. You can also use a wet-dry vacuum to remove water from the sump area.

faq.q: Why does water back up from the sink into the dishwasher?
faq.a: This usually means the drain hose lacks a high loop or the check valve has failed, allowing sink water to flow back into the dishwasher.

title: Filter Blockage Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher's filtration system is severely clogged, restricting water flow.
fullDescription: Error E22 indicates that the internal filters are excessively clogged, severely restricting water circulation. The flow sensor detects reduced flow due to the blockage. This typically happens when filters have not been cleaned for an extended period, or a large amount of food debris has entered the system at once.

causes.cause: Filters have not been cleaned and are heavily clogged
Large food particles bypassed the pre-filter
Broken glass or debris lodged in filter housing
Filter assembly is damaged or not seated correctly

fixSteps.title: Remove and clean all filters
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack and spray arm. Twist and remove the cylindrical fine filter, then lift out the flat metal coarse filter. Rinse both under running water, using a soft brush to remove all food debris and grease.

fixSteps.title: Clean the filter housing
fixSteps.instruction: With the filters removed, inspect the filter housing in the sump. Remove any debris accumulated there. Check for broken glass or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Reinstall filters properly
fixSteps.instruction: Place the flat coarse filter back first, then insert and twist-lock the cylindrical fine filter until it clicks into place. Improper seating can allow debris to bypass the filter.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run a hot cycle empty with dishwasher cleaner to flush the entire system of any remaining debris and grease.

repairTime: 15 minutes to 30 minutes (plus cleaning cycle time)
whenToCallPro: E22 is almost always resolved by filter cleaning. If the error persists after thorough cleaning, there may be a deeper blockage in the sump requiring professional attention.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E22 filter clogged error fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the filters?
faq.a: Bosch recommends cleaning filters after every wash or at minimum once a week for regular use. Monthly deep cleaning is advised.

faq.q: Do I need to pre-rinse dishes for a Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: You do not need to pre-rinse, but scrape off large food particles. The filters handle small debris, but excessive food can overwhelm them.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without filters?
faq.a: No. Running without filters allows food debris to damage the pump and clog the drain. Always ensure filters are properly installed.

title: Drain Pump Continuous Operation
shortDescription: The drain pump is running continuously without stopping.
fullDescription: Error E23 indicates the drain pump is running non-stop. This can be triggered by a stuck relay on the control board, a shorted drain pump wire, or the AquaStop system continuously trying to remove water from the base pan. The control board has detected the pump running outside of its expected cycle parameters.

causes.cause: Water in the base pan triggering continuous drain (AquaStop)
Control board relay for drain pump is stuck closed
Drain pump wiring is shorted
Drain pump motor is stuck in run mode

symptoms: Drain pump runs even when dishwasher is idle
Dishwasher will not start a wash cycle
May see E15 error alternating with E23

fixSteps.title: Check for water in base pan
fixSteps.instruction: This is often caused by E15 (AquaStop). Check the base pan for water. If water is present, tilt the dishwasher forward to drain it and find the leak source.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect and reconnect power
fixSteps.instruction: If no water is in the base pan, disconnect power at the breaker for 10 minutes. This may reset the relay. Reconnect and see if the pump stops.

fixSteps.title: Check pump wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the wiring from the control board to the drain pump for any shorts, pinched wires, or melted insulation that could keep the pump energized.

fixSteps.title: Test control board relay
fixSteps.instruction: If the pump runs immediately when power is restored (even with the pump disconnected from the board showing voltage on the relay output), the control board relay is stuck and the board needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the control board relay is suspected, professional repair is recommended as board-level diagnosis requires specialized equipment.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E23 drain pump continuous running fix

faq.q: Why does the drain pump keep running?
faq.a: The most common reason is water in the base pan activating the AquaStop system. The pump runs to remove this water. Check for and address any leaks.

faq.q: Can continuous pump running damage the motor?
faq.a: Yes, running the drain pump continuously can overheat and burn out the motor. Address the cause promptly.

faq.q: Is E23 related to E15?
faq.a: Often yes. E15 (AquaStop activation) triggers continuous drain pump operation, which can then generate E23. Fixing the E15 leak usually resolves E23.

title: Drain Hose Clogged or Kinked
shortDescription: Water cannot drain from the dishwasher due to a blockage or restriction in the drain hose.
fullDescription: Error E24 is one of the most common Bosch dishwasher errors. It indicates the dishwasher is unable to drain water from the tub within the expected time. The most frequent cause is a kinked drain hose, a clogged garbage disposal connection, or a blockage in the drain line. This error can also appear if the drain hose is installed incorrectly.

causes.cause: Drain hose is kinked or bent sharply
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed
Drain hose or sink drain is clogged
Drain pump filter is clogged with debris
Drain check valve is stuck closed

symptoms: Standing water remains in the bottom of the tub
Dishes are not clean with dirty water left on them

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher forward enough to inspect the drain hose. Look for kinks, sharp bends, or compression. Straighten any kinks. The hose should rise to a high loop under the counter before descending to the drain connection.

fixSteps.title: Check the disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal to clear it. If newly installed, check that the knockout plug was removed from inside the disposal's dishwasher inlet.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter and sump
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filters. Clear any debris from the sump drain area. Check for glass, bones, or other obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Flush the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the disposal or drain connection. Place the end in a bucket and run a drain cycle to check for flow. If water does not flow, disconnect the hose entirely and flush it with running water to clear any blockage.

whenToCallPro: If you have cleared all visible blockages and the drain hose is not kinked but E24 persists, the drain pump may be failing and needs professional evaluation.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E24 drain clogged error fix

faq.q: E24 is the most common Bosch dishwasher error. Why?
faq.a: Because drain hose kinks and disposal knockout plugs are extremely common issues, especially after installation or kitchen remodeling.

faq.q: What is the garbage disposal knockout plug?
faq.a: When a garbage disposal is manufactured, the dishwasher drain inlet has a solid plug. If you connect a dishwasher, this plug must be knocked out from inside the disposal before connecting the drain hose.

faq.q: How high should the drain hose loop be?
faq.a: The drain hose should loop as high as possible under the counter (at least 20 inches from the floor) before connecting to the drain. This prevents siphoning.

title: Drain Pump Impeller Blocked
shortDescription: The drain pump cover or impeller area is blocked, preventing the pump from operating.
fullDescription: Error E25 specifically points to a blockage at the drain pump impeller or pump cover. Unlike E24 which indicates downstream blockage, E25 means something is physically preventing the pump from spinning. This is usually debris that has passed through the filters and lodged in the pump.

causes.cause: Debris (glass, toothpick, seed) jammed in the pump impeller
Pump cover is not properly seated after cleaning
Broken impeller blade
Grease buildup on pump impeller

symptoms: Drain pump makes humming but no draining sound
May hear clicking from pump area

fixSteps.title: Access the drain pump cover
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filters. Locate the drain pump cover in the sump area. It usually has a cover that twists or snaps off. Carefully remove it.

fixSteps.title: Clear the impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the pump chamber for any debris blocking the impeller. Common items include broken glass, fruit pits, toothpicks, and small bones. Carefully remove any obstructions. Wear gloves to protect against glass.

fixSteps.title: Check impeller rotation
fixSteps.instruction: Gently spin the impeller by hand. It should rotate freely. If it feels gritty or stiff, clean around it with a soft brush. If a blade is broken, the pump needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the pump cover
fixSteps.instruction: Replace the pump cover securely. An improperly seated cover can also trigger E25. Ensure it clicks or locks into place. Reinstall filters, spray arm, and rack.

whenToCallPro: If the impeller is broken or the pump motor is seized, replacement is needed. A professional can handle this efficiently.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E25 drain pump blocked impeller fix

faq.q: What is the difference between E24 and E25?
faq.a: E24 typically indicates a downstream drain blockage (hose, disposal), while E25 specifically points to something blocking the pump impeller inside the dishwasher.

faq.q: How do I prevent debris from reaching the pump?
faq.a: Always scrape dishes before loading. Keep the filters clean and properly installed. Check that the filter assembly is correctly seated after each cleaning.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher if the pump cover is missing?
faq.a: No. The pump cover ensures proper water flow to the impeller. Running without it can cause poor drainage and potentially damage the pump.

title: Pressure Switch or Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The pressure switch or analog pressure sensor is not reading correctly.
fullDescription: Error E26 indicates the water level pressure sensor (or pressure switch) is not providing reliable readings to the control board. This sensor measures water level in the tub to control fill and drain operations. An E26 fault can cause both overfilling and underfilling issues.

causes.cause: Pressure sensor is faulty
Air trap or pressure chamber is clogged
Pressure hose is cracked, kinked, or disconnected
Wiring to the pressure sensor is damaged

symptoms: May trigger E03 or E05 intermittently

fixSteps.title: Clean the pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the pressure chamber (air trap) in the sump area. It is a small cylindrical chamber connected to the pressure sensor by a hose. Clean out any debris, grease, or scale from the chamber.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Check the rubber hose connecting the pressure chamber to the pressure sensor. Ensure it is not cracked, kinked, or disconnected at either end. Replace if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the pressure sensor and blow gently into the pressure hose. You should hear the sensor click or, for analog sensors, measure a resistance change with a multimeter. No response means the sensor is faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor is faulty, disconnect the hose and wiring connector. Remove the mounting hardware and install the new sensor.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the pressure system and replacing the sensor does not resolve E26, the control board's pressure sensor input circuitry may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E26 pressure switch error fix

faq.q: What does the pressure switch do?
faq.a: The pressure switch measures the water level in the tub by sensing air pressure in a sealed chamber. As water rises, it compresses air in the chamber, which the switch or sensor detects.

faq.q: Can a dirty pressure chamber cause E26?
faq.a: Yes. Grease and debris in the pressure chamber can interfere with accurate pressure readings. Regular cleaning prevents this.

faq.q: Is E26 the same as the float switch?
faq.a: No. The float switch (related to E15/AquaStop) is a separate flood protection device. The pressure sensor (E26) is used for normal water level regulation.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The control board has detected that the incoming voltage is outside the acceptable range.
fullDescription: Error E27 indicates the control board's voltage monitoring circuit has detected supply voltage that is too high or too low. Bosch dishwashers typically require 120V AC (in North America) and will fault if voltage drops below approximately 100V or exceeds 130V. This protects sensitive electronics from damage.

causes.cause: Home electrical voltage is too low (brownout condition)
Loose or corroded connections at the junction box
Shared circuit with high-draw appliances causing voltage drops
Utility power supply issue in the area

symptoms: Error appears more during peak usage hours
Lights in the kitchen may flicker or dim

fixSteps.title: Check outlet voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Using a multimeter, measure voltage at the dishwasher junction box or outlet. It should read between 110-125V AC. Readings outside this range confirm a power supply issue.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power first. Inspect the wiring connections at the dishwasher junction box. Tighten any loose wire nuts and check for corroded or burned wires.

fixSteps.title: Test at different times
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage was borderline, test again during different times of day. Peak usage times (evening) may show lower voltage. This helps identify if it is a household or utility issue.

fixSteps.title: Ensure dedicated circuit
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the dishwasher is on its own dedicated 15A or 20A circuit as required by code. Sharing a circuit with other high-draw appliances can cause voltage drops.

whenToCallPro: If voltage at the outlet is consistently low or unstable, contact an electrician to evaluate your home's electrical system and the utility supply.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E27 voltage error power supply fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E27?
faq.a: Yes, both low voltage (brownout) and high voltage (surge) can trigger E27. A surge protector or voltage regulator may help if power quality is poor.

faq.q: Does the dishwasher need its own circuit?
faq.a: Yes. Bosch recommends a dedicated 15A or 20A circuit for the dishwasher. Sharing a circuit with a garbage disposal or other appliances can cause voltage issues.

faq.q: Will E27 damage my dishwasher?
faq.a: E27 is actually a protection mechanism. The dishwasher stops to prevent damage from abnormal voltage. The error itself does not cause damage.

title: Turbidity Sensor Error
shortDescription: The turbidity (water clarity) sensor is malfunctioning or reading out of range.
fullDescription: Error E28 indicates a fault with the turbidity sensor, which measures water clarity during the wash cycle. This sensor helps the dishwasher determine when dishes are clean by measuring how much light passes through the water. A faulty sensor may cause the dishwasher to run excessively long cycles or not clean properly.

causes.cause: Turbidity sensor lens is coated with grease or scale
Turbidity sensor has failed electronically
Wiring to the sensor is damaged
Sensor connector is corroded

symptoms: Wash cycles run much longer than expected
Auto-sensing cycles do not adjust properly

fixSteps.title: Clean the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: The turbidity sensor is located in the sump area. Remove the filters and locate the small sensor with a clear lens. Clean the lens carefully with a soft cloth and mild detergent to remove grease and mineral deposits.

fixSteps.title: Run a cleaning cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Run a hot empty cycle with dishwasher cleaner to flush the system. Heavy grease buildup throughout the system can affect sensor readings.

fixSteps.title: Test the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning does not resolve E28, the sensor may need replacement. Access the sensor from the sump area and check its electrical connector for corrosion. Test resistance or voltage output per the service manual specifications.

fixSteps.title: Replace the turbidity sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the sensor's wiring harness, remove the retaining clip or screws, and install the new sensor. Ensure the lens is clean on the new sensor before installation.

whenToCallPro: If the sensor tests fine but E28 persists, the control board input circuit may need professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E28 turbidity sensor error fix

faq.q: What is a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: A turbidity sensor measures water clarity by shining a light through the water and measuring how much passes through. Clear water means clean dishes; cloudy water means more washing is needed.

faq.q: Does every Bosch dishwasher have a turbidity sensor?
faq.a: Most modern Bosch dishwashers with auto-sensing cycles have turbidity sensors. Basic models without auto-sensing may not have one.

faq.q: Can I manually select cycles if the turbidity sensor fails?
faq.a: Yes, you can use non-auto cycles (like Normal or Heavy) that run for a fixed time regardless of water clarity. However, you should still repair the sensor.

title: Communication Error Between Control Boards
shortDescription: The main control board and the display/interface board cannot communicate.
fullDescription: Error E29 indicates a communication failure between the main control board (located in the base of the dishwasher) and the user interface board (in the door panel). These boards communicate over a data bus and E29 means this communication link has been lost or is experiencing errors.

causes.cause: Ribbon cable between door boards is damaged or loose
Door hinge flexing has damaged the ribbon cable over time
User interface board has failed
Main control board communication circuit has failed

symptoms: E29 error on display (may flash briefly before screen goes blank)
Buttons on the control panel are unresponsive
Display shows random characters or is blank
Dishwasher starts but controls become unresponsive mid-cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for 10 minutes. This can reset the communication bus. Reconnect and check if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Open the inner door panel. Locate the ribbon cable that connects the interface board to the main control board. Check for any visible damage, especially near the hinge area where it flexes. Reseat both connectors firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check connectors for corrosion
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all board connectors for corrosion or oxidation. Clean contacts with electrical contact cleaner if needed.

fixSteps.title: Replace the ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: If the ribbon cable shows damage, replace it. Route the new cable carefully to avoid the hinge stress point.

whenToCallPro: If the ribbon cable is good, one of the two boards has likely failed. Board-level diagnosis requires professional skills.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E29 communication error control board fix

faq.q: Can door opening and closing cause E29?
faq.a: Over time, the repeated flexing of the door can stress and eventually break the ribbon cable between the two boards, causing E29.

faq.q: Is E29 expensive to fix?
faq.a: If it is just the ribbon cable, the fix is inexpensive ($15-$35 for the part). If a control board has failed, costs rise significantly ($100-$350).

faq.q: Can I fix a damaged ribbon cable?
faq.a: Ribbon cables should be replaced rather than repaired. Soldering ribbon cable conductors is impractical for most homeowners.

title: Control Board Software Error
shortDescription: The main control board has encountered a software or firmware error.
fullDescription: Error E30 indicates an internal software or firmware error in the main control board. The processor has encountered an unexpected condition that it cannot recover from automatically. This may be caused by a power glitch corrupting memory, a firmware bug, or hardware degradation on the board.

causes.cause: Power surge or glitch corrupted control board memory
Control board firmware has become corrupted
Control board hardware is degrading
Electrical noise from other appliances interfering

symptoms: Random cycle behavior before error appeared
Error may appear immediately on power-up

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power at the circuit breaker for at least 15 minutes. This gives the control board capacitors time to fully discharge and allows the processor to cold-start. Restore power and test.

fixSteps.title: Try the button reset sequence
fixSteps.instruction: Some Bosch models allow a reset by pressing and holding specific buttons. Try holding the Start button for 3-5 seconds, or pressing the Power button, then Start, then Power again within 3 seconds.

fixSteps.title: Check for voltage issues
fixSteps.instruction: Verify outlet voltage is stable at 120V AC. Voltage fluctuations can cause software errors. Consider a surge protector for the dishwasher circuit.

fixSteps.title: Control board replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If E30 persists after multiple resets and stable power is confirmed, the control board may need replacement or professional firmware reprogramming.

whenToCallPro: If a power reset does not clear E30, the control board likely needs professional diagnosis and possible replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E30 control board software error reset fix

faq.q: Will a power reset fix E30?
faq.a: Often yes. E30 caused by a power glitch typically clears with a thorough power reset (15+ minutes disconnected). If it returns, the board may have a deeper issue.

faq.q: Can I update the firmware on my Bosch dishwasher?
faq.a: Firmware updates for Bosch dishwashers require a professional service technician with specialized diagnostic equipment. This is not a DIY task.

faq.q: Should I use a surge protector for my dishwasher?
faq.a: While not required, a whole-house surge protector or a dedicated surge protector on the dishwasher circuit can help prevent voltage-related control board issues.

title: Control Board Hardware Failure
shortDescription: The main control board has detected an unrecoverable hardware failure.
fullDescription: Error E31 is a critical error indicating the main control board has experienced a hardware failure that cannot be resolved by resetting. This typically means a component on the board (capacitor, relay, processor, or other IC) has failed permanently. The dishwasher will not operate until the board is replaced.

causes.cause: Component failure on the main control board
Power surge damaged control board circuitry
Water damage to the control board from a leak
Age-related capacitor or solder joint failure

symptoms: No response to any button presses after reset
Error appears immediately on power-up every time
May see visible burn marks or smell from the control board area

fixSteps.title: Attempt power reset
fixSteps.instruction: As a first step, disconnect power for 15 minutes and try again. While E31 usually indicates a permanent failure, occasionally a full discharge resolves it.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the inner door panel to access the control board. Look for any visible signs of damage: burned components, swollen capacitors, discolored areas, or moisture/corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture damage
fixSteps.instruction: If there are signs of water damage, identify and fix the source of moisture before installing a new board. Check the door seal and all connections in the door.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control board
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct replacement board. Transfer all wiring connectors from the old board to the new one, matching each connector to its position. The new board should come pre-programmed for the dishwasher model.

whenToCallPro: Control board replacement is recommended as a professional job. Incorrect connector placement can damage the new board. A technician can also verify no other components were damaged.
videoSearchQuery: Bosch dishwasher E31 control board replacement

faq.q: Is it worth replacing the control board or should I buy a new dishwasher?
faq.a: If the dishwasher is less than 7-8 years old and otherwise in good condition, board replacement ($250-$550 including labor) is usually worthwhile. For older units, weigh the cost against a new dishwasher.

faq.q: Can the control board be repaired instead of replaced?
faq.a: Some electronics repair shops specialize in appliance board repair and may be able to fix individual components for less than a new board. Search for appliance circuit board repair services.

faq.q: Do I need to program the new control board?
faq.a: Most replacement boards come pre-programmed for specific model groups. Ensure you order the exact part number for your model. Some may require technician programming.
